task_completion.description: Verify the minimum password length is set to 12 in the OpenClinica configuration file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal showing a text editor (like nano or vim) or a command output (like cat or grep) displaying the contents of '/usr/local/tomcat/webapps/OpenClinica/WEB-INF/classes/datainfo.properties' inside the 'oc-app' container, with a property for password length (e.g., passwd.length.min) set to 12.

task_completion.description: Verify the password expiration is set to 90 days in the OpenClinica configuration file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal showing the 'datainfo.properties' file being edited or viewed, with a property for password expiration (e.g., passwd.expiration.days) explicitly set to 90.

task_completion.description: Verify the support email is set to 'audit-support@clinical-trials.org' in the OpenClinica configuration file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal showing the 'datainfo.properties' file being edited or viewed, with the support email property (e.g., support.email) set to 'audit-support@clinical-trials.org'.

task_completion.description: Verify the database was updated to force a password reset for user 'mrivera'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal showing interaction with the 'oc-postgres' database (e.g., using psql). There should be an UPDATE SQL statement targeting the 'user_account' table for the user 'mrivera', setting their password timestamp/date to a value at least 90 days in the past, followed by a success confirmation (e.g., 'UPDATE 1').

task_completion.description: Verify the OpenClinica application container was restarted to apply the new configurations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command executing a restart of the application container, such as 'docker restart oc-app', and returning the container name as confirmation.

task_completion.description: Verify the OpenClinica application is accessible after the restart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser window open to the OpenClinica application URL (typically localhost:8080/OpenClinica), successfully displaying the login screen or application interface without connection errors.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually modified the file inside the correct Docker container.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the file edits were performed via 'docker exec' into the 'oc-app' container, rather than modifying a local dummy file on the host machine.

integrity.description: Ensure the SQL update command was actually executed against the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the PostgreSQL prompt and execution confirmation (like 'UPDATE 1') rather than just typing the command into a text editor or leaving it unexecuted in the prompt.

integrity.description: Ensure the application access check is genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The web browser should show the actual OpenClinica interface loaded from the local server (check the address bar), not a static image, a search results page, or an error page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target user is 'mrivera'. Expected minimum password length is 12. Expected password expiration is 90 days. Expected support email is 'audit-support@clinical-trials.org'. The database update must target the 'user_account' table for 'mrivera' and set the password timestamp to a date at least 90 days in the past to trigger immediate expiration.
